rate

(/odie/reactions/reaction/rate)

Reaction rate constants are in units that depend on the "arity" of the reaction; i.e. the number of reactants that participate in the reaction. The rate constant of a unary (decay) reaction is in Hz and amounts to the reciprocal of the mean lifetime of a reactant molecule. The rate constant of a binary reaction is in (Hz)(l)/(mol).

AttributesChildren
  • value
